reasoning: The impact probability for asteroid 2024 YR4 hitting Earth peaked at 3.1% in February 2025. However, subsequent observations effectively ruled out an Earth impact, dropping the probability to near zero. Consequently, YR4 is no longer considered a threat to Earth and its cumulative impact probability on the Sentry list is zero (if listed at all). While recent James Webb Space Telescope observations raised the asteroid's chance of hitting the Moon to 3.8%, this remains below the 4% threshold and does not correspond to an Earth impact. Furthermore, the asteroid will become unobservable from Earth starting in late April 2025, meaning no new data will emerge to push the probability higher before the June 30, 2025 resolution date. Therefore, the chance of the Sentry page showing a >= 4% Earth impact probability by the deadline is essentially zero.
